titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 628

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=1 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.291812+00 | translation moa=2 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.277371+00

div.p:
Alexander Dei Gratia Rex Scottorum Omnibus Probis Hominibus Tocius Terre Sue Clericis Et Laycis Salutem . Sciatis
nos concessisse et hac carta nostra confirmasse illam quietam clamationem et abiurationem . quam Patricius filius Comitis Patricii
fecit pro se et Heredibus suis deo et sancto Cuthberto et sancte Ebbe et priori et Monachis Dunnelmensibus apud Coldingham’ . deo
seruientibus et in perpetuum seruituris de uilla de Swinewd’ et toto dominico eiusdem uille cum omnibus pertinentiis suis et toto
iure et omni genere iuris et clamio . si quod habuit uel habere potuit in eadem uilla . tam in dominicis quam hominibus . seruitiis
et homagiis . boscis et planis . terris cultis et incultis . et omnibus aliis ad dictam uillam et dictum dominicum pertinentibus . Ita
Libere et Quiete . Plenarie et honorifice . sicut carta eiusdem Patricii dictis Priori et Monachis Dunnelmensibus apud
Coldingham’ plenius confecta  iuste testatur. Saluo seruitio nostro. Testibus Waltero Olifard’ Iusticiario Laodonie . Iohanne
deMaccuswel Camerario . Dauid Marscallo . Rogero Auenel . Ranulfo de Bonekyl Radulfo de Campania . Thoma
filio Ranulfi . Bernardo Fraser’ . Thoma de Haya . Willelmo de Bonekyl . apud Rokysburc’ Vicesimo Nono die
Marcij . Anno regni domini regis Septimo decimo.

Alexander by the grace of God king of Scots to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Know that we have granted and confirmed by this our charter the quitclaim and abjuration that Patrick son of Earl Patrick has made for himself and his own heirs to God, Saint Cuthbert, Saint Æbbe, and to the prior and monks of Durham at Coldingham who are serving God and, in perpetuity, those who will serve God, of the toun of Swinwood and the whole demesne of that same toun with all its pertinents and whole right, and every kind of right and claim, if he has had or was able to have such in the same toun, in demesnes as well as in men, services and homages, woods and plains, lands cultivated and uncultivated, and all other things pertaining to the said toun and the said demesne. As freely and quietly, fully and honourably, as the charter of the same Patrick made more fully for the said prior and monks of Durham at Coldingham justly declares. Saving our service. As witnesses: Walter Oliphant, justiciar of Lothian; John of Maxwell, chamberlain; David the marischal; Roger Avenel; Ranulf of Bunkle; Ralph de Champagne; Thomas son of Ranulf; Bernard Fraser; Thomas de la Haye; William of Bunkle. At Roxburgh, on the twenty-ninth day of March, in the seventeenth year of the reign of the Lord king.
